prompt: Continue this story: The lighthouse had been dark for three years when, one foggy night, its lamp flickered back to life.
Write the opening paragraph of a novel set in a city where it has rained continuously for a decade.
Describe an autumn morning in a small mountain village, focusing on sound and smell.
Write a short reflective passage about finding an old photograph of someone you no longer remember.
Continue: She opened the letter she had been avoiding for weeks, and the first line made her laugh out loud.
Compose a vivid description of a marketplace on another planet, from the perspective of a first-time visitor.
Write a melancholy paragraph about the last train leaving a station that is about to close forever.
Begin a tale about a clockmaker who discovers one of their clocks runs backward only at midnight.
Continue: The map was hand-drawn, the ink faded, and it marked an island that no chart had ever recorded.
Describe a thunderstorm rolling across an empty prairie at dusk.
Write the first paragraph of a mystery that begins with a single wet footprint inside a locked room.
Continue: He had promised never to return to the house on Alder Street, and yet here he was, key in hand.
Write a tender scene between an old fisherman and the gull that visits his boat each morning.
Describe the moment a child first sees the ocean, in rich sensory detail.
Begin a story set in a library where the books whisper to anyone who stays after closing.
Continue: The garden had grown wild in her absence, but one rosebush bloomed exactly where she had planted it.
Write a short passage capturing the feeling of returning to your childhood home as an adult.
Describe a bustling night market through the eyes of a hungry traveler with one coin left.
Begin a tale about a town that wakes one morning to find the sea has vanished overnight.
Continue: The violin in the attic had no strings, and yet at three in the morning it began to play.
Write a reflective paragraph about watching the last leaf fall from a tree outside your window.
Describe a desert at noon so vividly the reader can feel the heat.
Begin a story about two strangers who share an umbrella and discover they are heading to the same funeral.
Continue: The bakery opened at dawn, but today the smell drifting from it was nothing anyone could name.
Write an evocative description of the northern lights as seen by someone who has waited years to witness them.
Begin a tale about a girl who collects the last words people say before they fall asleep.
Continue: The elevator stopped between floors, the lights dimmed, and a voice that wasn't the intercom said hello.
Describe a snowfall in a city at midnight, when the streets are finally empty.
Write a short passage about an astronaut watching Earth rise over the lunar horizon for the first time.
Begin a story in a village where everyone is born knowing the day they will leave it.
Continue: She found the diary buried in the garden, its pages dry despite forty years underground.
Describe the inside of an abandoned amusement park reclaimed by the forest.
Write a wistful paragraph about the smell of a grandparent's kitchen.
Begin a tale about a postman who delivers letters to people who no longer exist.
Continue: The tide went out that evening and did not come back, leaving the boats stranded under a green sky.
Describe a single candle burning in a vast, dark cathedral.
Write the opening of a story about a musician who can only remember a song by giving away a memory.
Continue: The train pulled into a station that wasn't on any timetable, and the only passenger to step off was me.
Describe the first warm day after a long winter, and how the whole street seems to exhale.
Begin a tale about a cartographer hired to map a forest that rearranges itself each night.
Write a reflective passage about saying goodbye to a car you've driven for twenty years.
Describe a lighthouse keeper's last night on the job before the light is automated.
Continue: Every mirror in the house showed the same room, except the one at the top of the stairs.
Begin a story about a baker whose bread carries the dreams of whoever eats it.
Write a vivid description of a coral reef from the point of view of a diver running low on air.
Continue: The old radio only picked up one station, and it had been broadcasting the same broadcast for fifty years.
Describe a meadow at the exact moment a summer storm breaks.
Begin a tale about a city built entirely on the backs of sleeping giants.
Write a quiet, moving passage about the last conversation between two old friends.
Continue: The seed she planted grew overnight into a tree that bore fruit shaped like tiny glass lanterns.
